name: React Aria
description: Unstyled React components and hooks for building accessible, high-quality UI with custom styles

description.value: Unstyled React components and hooks for building accessible, high-quality UI with custom styles
description.comment: Adobe's headless component library providing behavior and accessibility without styling constraints

repository.comment: Part of the React Spectrum monorepo which includes React Aria, React Stately, and React Spectrum

github-stars.comment: 14.6k stars for the react-spectrum monorepo as of December 2025

framework.comment: React-based library with hooks that can be used across platforms including React Native

styling-approach.comment: Completely unstyled, style-free out of the box, allowing any styling solution (CSS, Tailwind, CSS-in-JS, etc.)

component-model.comment: Headless component library providing behavior and accessibility without default styles

typescript.comment: First-class TypeScript support with full type definitions, TSX examples throughout documentation

ssr-compatible.comment: Full SSR support for Next.js, Remix, Gatsby. React 18+ works out of the box, React 16/17 requires SSRProvider

rsc-compatible.comment: React Server Components compatible with specific tooling like LocalizedStringProvider and @react-aria/optimize-locales-plugin for Next.js

design-system.comment: Headless library with no design system; users implement their own. Adobe Spectrum Design System uses React Aria under the hood

dark-mode.comment: No built-in dark mode system as it's a headless library; users implement dark mode in their styling layer

theming-system.comment: No built-in theming system - unstyled components allow complete custom theming via your chosen styling solution

css-variables.comment: No built-in CSS variables; developers implement their own styling approach and can use CSS variables if desired

design-tokens.comment: No design tokens provided - headless library focused on behavior and accessibility

rtl-support.comment: Comprehensive RTL support for right-to-left languages including RTL layout and interactions (e.g., keyboard navigation)

component-count.comment: Over 50 distinct components as of December 2025, including recent additions like Toast, Tree, Virtualizer, and Autocomplete

text-input.comment: TextField component with Input, Label, and Text sub-components for accessible text input

textarea.comment: TextArea component for multi-line text input

select.comment: Custom Select component with accessibility, keyboard navigation, and ListBox integration

multi-select.comment: Multi-selection supported via ListBox with selectionMode='multiple' and TagGroup for tag-based selection

combobox.comment: ComboBox component with searchable/filterable select and autocomplete capabilities

checkbox.comment: Checkbox and CheckboxGroup components with indeterminate state support

radio.comment: Radio and RadioGroup components for radio button groups

switch-toggle.comment: Switch component for boolean toggle values

slider.comment: Slider component with single or dual-thumb support for range selection

number-input.comment: NumberField component with increment/decrement controls and keyboard input

tag-input.comment: TagGroup component for multiple tag/token entry with add/remove functionality

pin-input.comment: No dedicated PIN input component; can be built using TextField with custom styling

color-picker.comment: Comprehensive color components: ColorPicker, ColorArea, ColorSlider, ColorWheel, ColorField, ColorSwatch, ColorSwatchPicker

date-picker.comment: DatePicker component with calendar-based date selection using @internationalized/date, supports 13 calendar systems

time-picker.comment: TimeField component for time selection, integrates with DatePicker for combined date-time selection

date-range-picker.comment: DateRangePicker with RangeCalendar for selecting start and end dates

file-upload.comment: FileTrigger component allows a user to access the file system with any pressable React Aria or React Spectrum component

dropzone.comment: DropZone component for drag-and-drop file upload with keyboard and screen reader support

input-groups.comment: Group component can combine inputs with icons, buttons, and text addons

input-with-dropdown.comment: ComboBox provides input with attached dropdown functionality

button-groups.comment: ToggleButton can be used in groups for segmented button controls

split-button.comment: No dedicated split button component, but can be composed with Button and MenuTrigger

table.comment: Table component with row selection, sorting, and column resizing support

data-table.comment: Table supports sorting, filtering, pagination, row selection, and drag-and-drop for advanced data table use cases

virtual-infinite-list.comment: Virtualizer component (GA as of March 2025) for virtualized scrolling of large lists

tree-view.comment: Tree component (GA as of March 2025) with hierarchical structure, keyboard navigation, drag-and-drop (June 2025)

list-view.comment: ListBox and GridList components for styled lists with keyboard navigation and selection

card.comment: No dedicated card component; headless library expects users to build custom layouts

tag-chip-pill.comment: Tag component within TagGroup for removable, clickable tags/chips

calendar.comment: Calendar and RangeCalendar components for month/week calendar views with internationalization support for 13 calendar systems

alert.comment: No dedicated alert component; use Toast for notifications or build custom alerts

toast-notification.comment: Toast component (GA as of March 2025) for temporary popup notifications with accessibility

progress-bar.comment: ProgressBar component for determinate and indeterminate progress indicators

progress-circle.comment: ProgressBar can be styled as circular progress using SVG

spinner-loader.comment: ProgressBar with isIndeterminate for spinner/loader functionality

modal-dialog.comment: Modal and Dialog components for centered overlay dialogs with focus management

drawer-sheet.comment: ModalOverlay can be customized for drawer/sheet patterns, official example with Framer Motion gesture-driven sheet

popover.comment: Popover component for floating content anchored to trigger elements

tooltip.comment: Tooltip component for hover/focus hint text with accessibility

dropdown-menu.comment: Menu and MenuTrigger components for action menus with keyboard navigation and typeahead

context-menu.comment: No dedicated context menu component, though Menu can be adapted for this use case

command-palette.comment: Official Command Palette example built with Autocomplete, styled with Tailwind CSS

tabs.comment: Tabs and TabList components for tab-based content switching

vertical-tabs.comment: Tabs support orientation='vertical' for side-oriented tab navigation

breadcrumb.comment: Breadcrumbs component for hierarchical path navigation

pagination.comment: No dedicated pagination component; typically built using Button groups with ARIA navigation

stepper.comment: No dedicated stepper component; custom Stepper example shown in documentation using context pattern

navigation-menu.comment: Menu component can be used for site navigation with dropdown support

sidebar.comment: No dedicated sidebar component; built using composition of other components

grid.comment: No layout grid component; headless library focuses on interactive components

divider.comment: Separator component for horizontal/vertical dividers with ARIA semantics

collapsible.comment: Disclosure component (GA November 2024) for expandable/collapsible content with hidden='until-found' API support

resizable.comment: Table supports column resizing; no general-purpose resizable panel component

splitter.comment: No dedicated splitter component for resizable split panes

rich-text-editors.comment: No official rich text editor integrations; React Aria components can be used with editors like Tiptap, Slate, or Lexical

form-libraries.comment: React Hook Form integration via Controller component; built-in validation with native HTML constraints, custom functions, and server-side errors

animation-libraries.comment: Official Framer Motion integration with documented examples (gesture-driven sheet, swipeable tabs) and styling guide section

icon-libraries.comment: Icon-agnostic; works with any React icon library (Lucide, Heroicons, Phosphor, etc.) by passing icons as children or props

date-libraries.comment: Uses @internationalized/date built by Adobe for date/time manipulation across locales and 13 calendar systems

aria-compliant.comment: Components implement WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide patterns with correct ARIA roles, attributes, and semantics

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard support including arrow keys, typeahead, multi-selection modifiers, landmark navigation, and focus indicators

screen-reader-tested.comment: Extensively tested across multiple screen readers (JAWS, NVDA, VoiceOver) and devices, including touch screen reader support

focus-management.comment: Automatic focus containment in overlays, focus restoration on close, intelligent focus movement, keyboard-only focus indicators

wcag-level.comment: Components follow WCAG guidelines; documentation references WCAG as resource for component design and building

accessibility-rating.comment: Exceptional accessibility implementation: WAI-ARIA compliant, extensively screen reader tested, full keyboard navigation, focus management, battle-tested in production. Note: 2023 audit found minor issues but none blocking for disabled users

bundle-size-full.comment: Monopackage approach makes 'full bundle' size not meaningful; tree-shaking ensures only used components are bundled. Single Button ~8.1KB minified with brotli

bundle-size-core.comment: Individual component sizes vary; components share dependencies (e.g., usePress) so total size amortizes with more components used

tree-shakeable.comment: Supports tree-shaking; bundlers should only include components you use. Historical tree-shaking issues have been addressed with PURE annotations

zero-runtime.comment: JavaScript runtime required for behavior, interactions, and accessibility features

lazy-loading.comment: Components can be lazy loaded with React.lazy() and dynamic imports; supports asynchronous loading in collections

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent documentation: all-new content with interactive CSS and Tailwind examples, comprehensive API reference, guides, full example apps, downloadable Storybook starter, StackBlitz integration, shadcn CLI support

interactive-docs.comment: Live component playground with interactive CSS and Tailwind examples, code sandbox integration, downloadable examples

storybook.comment: Downloadable Storybook starter kit with pre-configured examples and CSS theme

figma-kit.comment: No official React Aria Figma kit from Adobe; however, Figma uses React Aria Components for their Simple Design System, and third-party kits like Untitled UI exist

cli-generator.comment: Supports shadcn CLI for adding components to projects; copy-paste workflow with ZIP downloads and StackBlitz integration

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: 1,264,248 weekly downloads for react-aria v3.44.0; react-aria-components has 697,357 weekly downloads (December 2025)

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained by Adobe with regular releases; latest December 16, 2025; 6,120 commits, 404 contributors

commercial-support.comment: No paid support or enterprise tier; open source project maintained by Adobe

style-override.comment: Multiple override methods: className (including function form with state), style prop, slots for named parts, data attributes for states

compound-components.comment: Extensive use of compound/composable components (e.g., Menu.Item pattern) with context-based composition

render-props.comment: Supports render props with state exposure; className and style accept functions receiving component states (isSelected, isHovered, etc.)

slots.comment: Named slots pattern for content injection (e.g., NumberField increment/decrement buttons); contexts provide slot-based composition

unstyled-mode.comment: Completely unstyled by default; entire library is unstyled/headless allowing full control over appearance
